meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Stop Abandoning Projects: The Student's Client Workflow Guide
content.seo_description: Struggling to finish your coding projects? Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your portfolio apps faster.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of scope management. By adopting a 'Client Workflow'—treating your personal project like a high-stakes contract—you force yourself to define deliverables, set rigid deadlines, and iterate in sprints. This structure transforms amorphous ideas into shippable, production-ready software.
content.intro:
You sit down to code, eyes locked on a blank terminal, fueled by the vision of a perfect app. Two weeks later, the momentum vanishes, replaced by a graveyard of half-baked folders and abandoned GitHub repositories. The 'tutorial hell' loop isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of process.

Professional engineers don't rely on motivation; they rely on frameworks. By simulating a client-developer relationship, you strip away the perfectionism that kills momentum. Today, we bridge the gap between 'idea' and 'shipped' using modern AI-driven tooling to keep you on rails until the finish line.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Product (MVP)
steps.description: Treat your project as a client request. Identify the three core features that solve the user's problem. Anything else is scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea: [Insert Idea]. Act as a product manager. Break it down into 3 core features and a 1-week delivery schedule.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a project roadmap file named ROADMAP.md based on these 3 MVP features'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, define how your data flows. This prevents mid-project refactoring which is the #1 project killer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a project with these core features. Provide SQL or Prisma definitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate the schema.prisma file based on the MVP requirements'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Use automated scaffolding to bypass the 'blank page' anxiety. Get the boilerplate running instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular project structure for a [Stack] application that supports the defined schema.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build one feature at a time. Do not move to feature B until feature A is fully tested and functional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the implementation for the user authentication module. Include error handling and validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement user authentication logic following the defined schema'

steps.title: Final QA and Documentation
steps.description: A project isn't finished until it's documented. Create a README that sells your work for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md for this project, highlighting the technical challenges solved and the stack used.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review current code and generate a README.md that showcases the project for recruiters'

pro_tips: Always commit after every successful small function; it creates a psychological 'win' streak.
Use a 'Done' list instead of a 'To-Do' list to visualize progress when you feel stuck.
Limit your daily coding session to 90 minutes of deep work to prevent burnout and feature bloat.
If you get stuck for more than 20 minutes, describe the problem to Claude Code; never sit in silence.
Treat your GitHub commit history as your client's audit log—consistency matters more than complexity.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway immediately, then share the live URL in our Discord to get feedback from other builders.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every line of code you write today is a step toward a professional career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
